title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Graph Retrieval
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a signed-off release evidence record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Lightweight Annotated Tags · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

Which action demonstrates Lightweight Annotated Tags without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Lightweight Annotated Tags condition decided your prediction.

content.value: Git Reference — Controls porcelain and plumbing command behavior, configuration, revision syntax, hooks, workflows, and recovery.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Lightweight Annotated Tags evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Lightweight Annotated Tags assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Release v5.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

Inspect the Signature Trust trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.

content.text: For Signature Trust, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Signature Trust precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Release v5.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A cryptographically valid signature proves the change is safe.

stimulus.title: Signature Trust case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Release v5.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A cryptographically valid signature proves the change is safe.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance. Artifact under review: a signed-off release evidence record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Signature Trust, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Signature Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
